questions.question: Why do you want to study in the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I want to study in the United States because {{why_this_field}}. The US education system offers superior {{field_of_study}} programs and opportunities that will help me achieve my career goals in a way that is not available in {{country_of_residence}}.
questions.guidance: Explain what the US offers that your home country cannot. Focus on educational advantages, not immigration intent.
questions.tooltip: Officers assess genuine educational motivation vs. immigration intent.
variations: What are your reasons for pursuing your education in the United States?
Could you explain your rationale for selecting the United States as your study destination?
Why have you decided to undertake your academic pursuits specifically in America?
What motivates your choice to study in the U.S. rather than elsewhere?

questions.question: What will be your major or field of study?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I will major in {{field_of_study}}.
questions.guidance: Be clear and specific about your major and intended specialization.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates a well-thought-out educational plan.
variations: Which academic program do you intend to pursue?
What is your chosen discipline for your studies here?
Can you specify your intended area of academic concentration?
What course of study will you be undertaking?

questions.question: Why did you choose this field of study?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I chose {{field_of_study}} because {{why_this_field}}. This field aligns with my interests and long-term career aspirations.
questions.guidance: Show genuine passion and connection to your field. Explain what drew you to it.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates thoughtful motivation for field selection.
variations: What led you to select this particular field for your studies?
Could you elaborate on your reasons for choosing this specific academic discipline?
What is your motivation behind pursuing this area of study?
Please explain your rationale for enrolling in this field of education.

questions.question: Tell us about your previous education and academic achievements.
questions.suggestedAnswer: I completed my {{current_education_level}} with a GPA of {{previous_gpa}}. {{previous_education_details}} This educational foundation has prepared me well for advanced studies.
questions.guidance: Describe your educational timeline, GPA, and notable achievements.
questions.tooltip: Officers verify academic preparation for higher education.
variations: Could you please summarize your prior educational background and any notable academic accomplishments?
Provide an overview of your academic history and significant achievements.
What has been your educational journey so far, and what are your key academic successes?
Please share details regarding your previous schooling and any academic merits you've earned.

questions.question: Who is your current employer?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I currently work as a {{current_employer_role}}.
questions.guidance: State your job title clearly.
questions.tooltip: Provides context for your work experience.
variations: Where are you currently employed?
Can you state the name of your present employer?
Who is the organization you are currently working for?
Could you tell us the name of your current company or institution of employment?

questions.question: How many years of work experience do you have?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I have {{work_experience_years}} years of professional work experience.
questions.guidance: Provide total years of relevant or full-time work experience.
questions.tooltip: Shows professional maturity and experience level.
variations: What is the total duration of your professional work experience?
Could you indicate the number of years you have worked professionally?
How long have you been employed in a professional capacity?
Please specify the total years of your relevant work experience?

questions.question: How does your work experience relate to your studies?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{work_experience_details}} This experience has given me valuable insights that align with my field of study and career goals.
questions.guidance: Connect your work experience directly to your proposed studies and future goals.
questions.tooltip: Shows alignment between past work and academic goals.
variations: In what ways does your professional experience connect with your intended course of study?
How will your past work experience inform or benefit your academic pursuits?
Can you explain the relevance of your work history to the program you wish to undertake?
Describe the alignment between your previous work and your chosen field of study.

questions.question: Why are you planning to continue your education in the United States? Can you not continue in your home country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: While I could continue my education in {{country_of_residence}}, the United States offers superior {{field_of_study}} programs, world-renowned professors, and research opportunities that are unmatched. This will significantly enhance my qualifications.
questions.guidance: Be honest about limitations in your home country while explaining US-specific advantages.
questions.tooltip: Officers want to understand why US education is specifically necessary.
variations: What are your specific reasons for choosing to pursue further education in the U.S., particularly if similar opportunities exist in your home country?
Could you explain your decision to advance your studies in America, and why your home country might not be suitable for these educational goals?
Why have you opted for the United States for your continued academic journey, especially considering the educational landscape in your native country?
Detail your rationale for seeking higher education in the U.S. at this stage, and clarify if comparable programs are available or suitable in your country of origin.

questions.question: How will this study program relate to your past work or studies?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My {{intended_degree}} at {{primary_university}} will advance my knowledge in {{field_of_study}} and enable me to {{post_graduation_job_plans}}. This represents a clear progression from my previous experience.
questions.guidance: Draw a clear connection between your past and future, showing coherent progression.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates a coherent educational and career plan.
variations: How does this academic program align with your previous professional experience or educational background?
In what way will this course of study build upon your prior work or academic pursuits?
Explain the connection between your proposed study program and your existing work history or educational foundation.
How do you see this study program contributing to or extending your past studies or career path?

questions.question: How many universities did you apply to?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I applied to {{total_universities_applied}} universities in total.
questions.guidance: State the exact number of universities you applied to.
questions.tooltip: Shows application selectivity and planning.
variations: To how many educational institutions did you submit applications?
What was the total number of universities you applied to for admission?
Could you state the quantity of applications you submitted to different schools?
Please inform us of the total count of universities where you sought admission.

questions.question: How many admission offers did you receive?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I received {{total_admits_received}} admission offers from universities.
questions.guidance: State the number of acceptance letters you received.
questions.tooltip: Indicates academic competitiveness and choices available.
variations: How many universities offered you admission?
From how many institutions did you receive an acceptance offer?
What was the total number of admission offers you secured?
Could you specify the number of admission letters you obtained?

questions.question: Were you waitlisted or rejected by any universities?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{waitlisted_universities ? 'Yes, I was waitlisted or rejected by some universities, but I received strong admissions from my preferred choices.' : 'I received admission from all universities I applied to or prioritized.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer honestly, but frame rejections positively.
questions.tooltip: Part of complete application picture.
variations: Did any universities place you on a waitlist or deny your application?
Were there any institutions that did not offer you admission, or put you on a waitlist?
Can you share if you received any waitlist notifications or rejections from other schools?
What were the outcomes of your other university applications?

questions.question: Why did you choose this university as your primary choice?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{why_primary_university}} This university stands out because of its program strength, faculty expertise, and alignment with my academic and career goals.
questions.guidance: Explain what makes this university unique for you and your field.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates genuine interest and informed decision-making.
variations: What are your primary reasons for selecting this particular university?
Could you explain why this institution stands out as your top choice?
Why have you decided to attend this specific university above others?
What led you to choose this university as the one you wish to attend?

questions.question: Have you visited the USA before?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{previous_us_visits ? 'Yes, I have visited the US previously, which helped me understand the environment.' : 'No, I have not visited the US before, but I have thoroughly researched {{primary_university}} through virtual tours, faculty websites, and student testimonials.'}}
questions.guidance: If visited, explain what you learned. If not, show detailed knowledge through research.
questions.tooltip: Officers assess your commitment and preparation.
variations: Do you have any previous travel experience to the United States?
Is this your first time coming to America?
Have you previously traveled to the U.S. for any reason?
Have you ever visited the United States prior to this?

questions.question: What do you know about your university's program and campus?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{university_knowledge}} I have researched the program curriculum, campus facilities, and community resources, and I am confident that this university is the right fit for me.
questions.guidance: Demonstrate detailed research about curriculum, facilities, professors, and campus life.
questions.tooltip: Shows genuine interest and preparation for arrival.
variations: Please describe what you've learned about the academic program and facilities at your university.
Can you share your understanding of the curriculum and campus environment of your chosen institution?
What information have you gathered regarding the specific program you'll be undertaking and the university campus?
Tell me what you know about the structure of your course of study and the physical aspects of the university.

questions.question: What do you know about professors or faculty in your field?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I have researched faculty in the {{field_of_study}} department at {{primary_university}}. I am particularly interested in learning from professors who specialize in that field.
questions.guidance: Mention specific professors or show knowledge of faculty research areas.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates genuine academic interest in the institution.
variations: Are you familiar with any specific faculty members or professors in your area of study at the university?
What knowledge do you have about the academic staff specializing in your field?
Could you tell me if you have researched any professors within your department?
Who are some of the notable faculty members or researchers in your chosen field at this university?

questions.question: What city and state is your university located in?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{primary_university}} is located in {{university_city_state}}.
questions.guidance: Mention city and state clearly.
questions.tooltip: Verifies university location knowledge.
variations: In which city and state is your chosen university situated?
Could you specify the geographic location, including the city and state, of your university?
Where exactly is your university located, in terms of city and state?
Can you name the city and state where your university is found?

questions.question: What do you know about the local area and community?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{university_knowledge}} The area offers various academic and recreational resources that will support my studies and personal growth.
questions.guidance: Show knowledge of the city, campus resources, and community opportunities.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates realistic understanding of where you'll study.
variations: What have you learned about the community and local surroundings of your university?
Could you tell me what you know about the city and neighborhood where your university is located?
What is your understanding of the area immediately surrounding the university campus?
Please describe what you know about the local environment and community near your university.

questions.question: Do you have your I-20 document?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{i20_number ? 'Yes, I have received my I-20 document.' : 'I am still awaiting my I-20 from the university, but I expect to receive it soon.'}}
questions.guidance: The I-20 is the official document required for F1 visa. Have you received it from the university?
questions.tooltip: Critical requirement for F1 visa processing.
variations: Is your Form I-20 readily available?
Can you present your Certificate of Eligibility for Nonimmigrant Student Status (Form I-20)?
Have you brought your I-20 form with you today?
Do you have the necessary I-20 document?

questions.question: What are your English proficiency test scores (TOEFL or IELTS)?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I achieved a score of {{ielts_score ? ielts_score : toefl_score}} on the {{english_proficiency_test}} exam, which demonstrates my readiness for university-level coursework in English.
questions.guidance: State your test scores confidently and explain what they mean for your academic readiness.
questions.tooltip: Test scores are mandatory verification of English proficiency.
variations: Could you please state your scores from your English language proficiency examination, such as TOEFL or IELTS?
What were your results on the standardized English assessment, like the TOEFL or IELTS?
Please provide your scores for either the TOEFL or IELTS test.
Can you confirm your scores for your English proficiency exam, be it TOEFL or IELTS?

questions.question: Did you take a standardized test (GRE, GMAT, SAT, or ACT)?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{standardized_test ? 'Yes, I took the {{standardized_test}} and scored {{standardized_test_score}}.' : 'No, a standardized test was not required for my program.'}}
questions.guidance: Many programs require GRE/GMAT for graduate and SAT/ACT for undergraduate programs.
questions.tooltip: Validates academic capability for your program level.
variations: Have you completed any standardized examinations like the GRE, GMAT, SAT, or ACT?
Were you required to take any standardized admissions tests such as the GRE, GMAT, SAT, or ACT?
Could you confirm if you have taken tests like the GRE, GMAT, SAT, or ACT?
Regarding your application, did you submit scores from standardized tests such as the GRE, GMAT, SAT, or ACT?

questions.question: What was your GPA in your previous education?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My previous GPA was {{previous_gpa}}, which demonstrates my consistent academic performance and preparation for graduate-level work.
questions.guidance: State your GPA on the same scale as your previous institution.
questions.tooltip: Shows academic preparation for US university standards.
variations: Could you state your Grade Point Average from your prior academic institution?
What was your academic average or equivalent during your most recent studies?
Please provide your GPA for your previous educational program?
How would you describe your academic performance in your last educational experience, perhaps by your GPA?

questions.question: Was your previous education conducted in English?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{medium_of_instruction ? 'Yes, my previous education was conducted in English, providing me a strong foundation.' : 'No, my education was not in English medium, but I have studied English extensively and achieved my proficiency scores.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer honestly about your educational medium.
questions.tooltip: Determines English proficiency level and need for support.
variations: Was English the primary language of instruction for your prior education?
Did you complete your previous studies through the medium of English?
Can you confirm if your previous coursework was taught in English?
To what extent was English used as the language of instruction in your last academic program?

questions.question: How confident are you in your English ability?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I would rate my English proficiency at {{english_confidence_rating}} out of 10. I have the foundation needed to succeed in English-medium coursework.
questions.guidance: Be honest about your comfort level with academic English.
questions.tooltip: Self-assessment of English readiness for US university.
variations: How would you rate your confidence in your English language skills?
To what extent do you feel prepared to handle academic work in English?
Are you comfortable with your current English proficiency for university-level studies?
Could you describe your level of self-assurance regarding your English communication skills for academic purposes?

questions.question: How will you manage studying in English at a US university?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{how_you_will_manage_english}} I am committed to utilizing all available resources to ensure my academic success.
questions.guidance: Explain your strategies such as tutoring, study groups, or academic support services.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates readiness to handle academic English challenges.
variations: What strategies do you plan to employ to succeed in an English-speaking academic environment at a US university?
How do you intend to adapt to the demands of coursework conducted entirely in English in the US?
Can you outline your approach to studying effectively in English at a university in the United States?
What measures will you take to ensure successful academic performance while studying in English here?

questions.question: How will you manage cultural and educational differences in the US?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I understand that US universities have different teaching styles, participation expectations, and independent study requirements. I am prepared to adapt by utilizing university support services, joining student organizations, and building relationships with professors during office hours.
questions.guidance: Show self-awareness about differences and concrete strategies to adapt.
questions.tooltip: Officers want confidence in your ability to thrive beyond just academics.
variations: What is your plan for adapting to the cultural and educational differences you may encounter in the United States?
How do you intend to navigate the distinct cultural and academic environments present in the US?
Could you explain your strategy for adjusting to the varying cultural norms and educational approaches in the US?
What steps will you take to successfully integrate into the cultural and educational landscape of the United States?

questions.question: Why pursue your degree in the US instead of Canada, Australia, or the UK?
questions.suggestedAnswer: The United States leads in {{field_of_study}} education and {{primary_university}} specifically offers {{why_primary_university}}. While other countries have good programs, the US aligns best with my career goals.
questions.guidance: Acknowledge other countries but explain why US is specifically best for you.
questions.tooltip: Shows thoughtful analysis of educational options.
variations: What are your specific reasons for choosing the United States for your degree, rather than Canada, Australia, or the UK?
Can you elaborate on your decision to study in the US, as opposed to other countries like Canada, Australia, or the UK?
Why have you opted for a degree program in the United States over similar opportunities in nations such as Canada, Australia, or the UK?
What makes the US your preferred destination for your academic pursuits when considering alternatives like Canada, Australia, or the UK?

questions.question: What will you do if you struggle academically in the first semester?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{academic_success_strategy}} I will also seek support from {{primary_university}} academic resources, meet with professors during office hours, form study groups, and utilize tutoring services if needed.
questions.guidance: Show proactive planning and knowledge of university support systems.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates realistic expectations and commitment to success.
variations: Should you encounter academic challenges during your initial semester, what steps do you intend to take?
If you find yourself facing academic difficulties in your first term, how will you address them?
What resources or actions would you utilize if your academic performance is not as expected in the first semester?
In the event of academic struggles in your first few months, what would be your course of action?

questions.question: Who is your financial sponsor?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My {{sponsor_relationship}} will sponsor my education.
questions.guidance: Identify your main source of financial support clearly.
questions.tooltip: Critical to establish who is funding your education.
variations: Could you please state the name of your financial guarantor?
Who will be providing the financial support for your studies?
Please identify the individual or organization funding your education?
Who is responsible for covering your educational expenses?

questions.question: What is your sponsor's occupation?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My sponsor works as a {{sponsor_occupation}}.
questions.guidance: Describe the primary source of their income clearly.
questions.tooltip: Assesses financial stability and income source.
variations: What is your financial sponsor's current profession?
Could you describe the nature of your sponsor's work or employment?
What type of work does your financial guarantor perform?
What is your sponsor's primary source of income or employment?

questions.question: What is your sponsor's annual income in USD?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My sponsor's annual income is approximately ${{sponsor_annual_income}}, which is sufficient to cover my education costs.
questions.guidance: Provide in USD, converting from local currency using current exchange rates.
questions.tooltip: Income is critical to prove financial capability for the full program duration.
variations: Could you state your sponsor's yearly earnings in US dollars?
What is the total annual income of your financial sponsor, expressed in USD?
Please provide the annual salary of your sponsor, denominated in US currency.
How much does your sponsor earn annually, in United States Dollars?

questions.question: Do you have proof of your sponsor's income?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{sponsor_income_proof ? 'Yes, I have prepared tax returns, salary certificates, and business documents as proof of income.' : 'I am working to gather the necessary documentation.'}}
questions.guidance: Prepare tax returns (last 2 years), salary certificates, or business documents.
questions.tooltip: Essential documents for visa officer verification.
variations: Can you present documentation verifying your sponsor's income?
Is evidence of your sponsor's financial earnings available?
Do you possess supporting documents for your sponsor's declared income?
Could you provide verification of your sponsor's annual earnings?

questions.question: What is the total estimated cost of your program?
questions.suggestedAnswer: The total estimated cost of my {{intended_degree}} program is approximately ${{total_program_cost}} per year, including tuition, fees, accommodation, and living expenses.
questions.guidance: Break down costs into components to show you've researched thoroughly.
questions.tooltip: Cost breakdown shows realistic planning.
variations: What is the approximate total expense for your entire academic program?
Could you specify the overall projected cost of your chosen course of study?
How much is the complete estimated expenditure for your educational program?
What is the comprehensive anticipated cost of attending your program?

questions.question: How much funding can you access for your education?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I have approximately ${{available_funds}} available for my education from my sponsor and personal resources.
questions.guidance: Include sponsor savings, family gifts, and any personal funds.
questions.tooltip: Must cover program cost to demonstrate financial capability.
variations: What is the total sum of funds you have readily available for your studies?
Could you indicate the amount of financial resources accessible for your educational expenses?
How much capital do you have at your disposal for your schooling?
Please state the total amount of funding you are able to utilize for your program.

questions.question: How do you plan to fund your entire education?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My sponsor will provide ${{available_funds}}, which covers my education costs. {{scholarship_status ? 'Additionally, my scholarship provides further support toward my education.' : ''}}
questions.guidance: Show complete picture: sponsor funds, scholarship (if any), and any personal contribution.
questions.tooltip: Officers need to see complete financing for all program years.
variations: Could you describe your strategy for financing your complete academic journey?
What is your comprehensive plan for covering the expenses of your education?
How do you intend to secure the necessary funds for your entire program of study?
Please explain your approach to funding all aspects of your education.

questions.question: Do you have proof of available funds?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{funds_availability_proof ? 'Yes, I have bank statements for the last 6 months and affidavits of support documenting available funds.' : 'I am preparing bank statements and other financial documents.'}}
questions.guidance: Prepare bank statements, affidavits of support, and investment documents.
questions.tooltip: Mandatory for I-20 and visa officer verification.
variations: Can you provide documentation to verify your available funds?
Do you have evidence supporting the amount of funds you have at your disposal?
Is there proof of your accessible financial resources?
Could you present verification of the funds you have available for your studies?

questions.question: Did you receive a scholarship?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{scholarship_status ? 'Yes, I received a scholarship from {{primary_university}}.' : 'No, I did not receive a scholarship, but my education is fully funded through my sponsor.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer honestly about scholarship status.
questions.tooltip: Scholarships strengthen financial capability profile.
variations: Have you been awarded any scholarships?
Were you granted a scholarship for your studies?
Did you secure any form of financial aid in the form of a scholarship?
Is there a scholarship associated with your enrollment?

questions.question: What is your scholarship amount?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I received a ${{scholarship_amount}} {{scholarship_type}} scholarship from {{primary_university}}.
questions.guidance: State the annual or total amount as specified in your scholarship letter.
questions.tooltip: Shows partial or full financial support from university.
variations: Could you state the value of your scholarship?
How much is your scholarship worth?
What is the monetary amount of the scholarship you received?
Please specify the total sum of your scholarship award.

questions.question: What type of scholarship did you receive?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My scholarship is {{scholarship_type}}, which significantly reduces my financial burden for my education.
questions.guidance: Specify if it's merit-based, need-based, full tuition, etc.
questions.tooltip: Shows academic excellence or financial strength.
variations: Can you describe the kind of scholarship you were awarded?
What is the nature of the scholarship you received?
Could you specify the category or type of your scholarship?
What sort of scholarship did you obtain?

questions.question: What if your sponsor cannot support you fully?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{funding_shortfall_plan}} I have researched alternative funding and identified multiple backup plans to ensure I can complete my education.
questions.guidance: Describe alternative sources: loans, additional family funds, part-time work, etc.
questions.tooltip: Backup plans show maturity and serious commitment.
variations: What is your contingency plan if your sponsor is unable to provide full support?
Should your sponsor be unable to cover all expenses, what alternative arrangements do you have?
If there's a shortfall in your sponsor's support, what is your backup funding strategy?
How would you address the financial gap if your sponsor cannot provide complete funding?

questions.question: What are your career goals after graduation?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{post_graduation_job_plans}} I plan to pursue this career after completing my {{intended_degree}}.
questions.guidance: Describe specific, realistic career goals aligned with your degree.
questions.tooltip: Clear post-graduation plans strengthen F1 visa application.
variations: What do you intend to do professionally once you complete your studies?
Could you outline your professional aspirations following the completion of your degree?
How do you envision your professional path developing after you graduate?
What are your plans for your career upon earning your degree?

questions.question: Do you plan to return to your home country after graduation?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{plan_to_return_home ? 'Yes, I plan to return to {{country_of_residence}} after graduation.' : 'I may pursue OPT or other visa options initially, but I intend to eventually return to {{country_of_residence}}.'}} I have strong ties to my home country including {{family_details}} and {{job_prospects_home_country}}.
questions.guidance: Be honest about your intent. Strong home country ties provide credibility.
questions.tooltip: Intent to return is critical for F1 visa officer assessment.
variations: Is it your intention to return to your native country subsequent to your graduation?
Do you have plans to reside in your home country after completing your program?
Will you be going back to your country of origin once your studies conclude?
After you graduate, do you intend to relocate back to your home country?

questions.question: What are your ties to your home country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{why_will_return}} I have family in {{country_of_residence}} and plan to {{post_graduation_job_plans}} there after completing my studies.
questions.guidance: Mention family, property, business, and career opportunities.
questions.tooltip: Establishes personal and financial ties to home country.
variations: Could you describe your connections to your country of origin?
What significant links do you maintain with your native country?
What factors bind you to your home country?
Can you explain what connections you have to your country of residence?

questions.question: Do you have close family in your home country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{family_in_home_country ? 'Yes, I have close family in {{country_of_residence}}: {{family_details}}' : 'Most of my family is abroad, but I have strong connections to {{country_of_residence}}.'}}
questions.guidance: Describe parents, siblings, or other family ties.
questions.tooltip: Critical indicator of home country attachment.
variations: Are your immediate family members currently residing in your country of origin?
Do you have any close relatives living in your native country?
Is your core family unit located in your home country?
Are there close family members who currently live in your country of nationality?

questions.question: Do you have property or assets in your home country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{property_or_assets_home ? 'Yes, my family owns property in {{country_of_residence}}, which represents significant family wealth.' : 'My family has financial investments and property in {{country_of_residence}} that strengthens our ties.'}}
questions.guidance: Land, house, or business ownership shows financial ties.
questions.tooltip: Financial ties strengthen return home commitment.
variations: Do you possess any real estate or financial assets within your country of origin?
Are you the owner of any property or substantial holdings in your native country?
Have you invested in any assets or real property in your home country?
Do you hold any significant possessions or financial interests in your country of residence?

questions.question: Are you considering staying in the USA after graduation?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{consider_staying_usa ? 'I may pursue {{usa_stay_plans}} to gain work experience in the US initially, but I intend to return to {{country_of_residence}}.' : 'My primary goal is to complete my studies and return to my home country.'}}
questions.guidance: If considering OPT/H1B, frame as temporary work experience before returning home.
questions.tooltip: Short-term US work followed by home country return demonstrates non-immigrant intent.
variations: Are you contemplating remaining in the United States once you complete your academic program?
Is it your intention to seek opportunities to extend your stay in the U.S. after graduating?
Have you thought about continuing to reside in the USA following your graduation?
Are you exploring options to stay in the United States subsequent to earning your degree?

questions.question: Do you have relatives or friends in the USA?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{relatives_in_usa ? 'Yes, I have {{relatives_usa_details}}, but my primary intent is to complete my studies and return to my home country where my main family is located.' : 'No, I do not have relatives or close friends in the USA, which reinforces my plan to return to {{country_of_residence}}.'}}
questions.guidance: If you have US relatives, emphasize that home country ties are stronger.
questions.tooltip: US relatives can be a concern - officers assess return intent.
variations: Are there any family members or acquaintances currently residing in the United States?
Do you have any relatives or close friends who live in the U.S.?
Do you have personal connections, such as family or friends, living within the USA?
Are any of your family or friends located here in the United States?

questions.question: Have you visited the USA before?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{previous_us_visits ? 'Yes, I have visited the USA before, which helped me understand the environment.' : 'No, I have not visited the USA before, but I am well-prepared through extensive research.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer honestly about travel history.
questions.tooltip: Checks travel experience and visa history.
variations: Have you previously traveled to the United States?
Have you made any prior visits to the U.S.?
Is this your first time traveling to the USA, or have you been here before?
Have you entered the United States on a previous occasion?

questions.question: When did you visit the USA and what was the purpose?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{previous_us_visit_details}} This visit helped me understand the US environment and strengthen my commitment to studying here.
questions.guidance: Provide approximate dates and mention campus tours or tourism.
questions.tooltip: Verifies legitimate travel and interest in US.
variations: Could you specify the dates and purpose of your previous visit(s) to the United States?
When did you last travel to the U.S., and what was the objective of that trip?
Please provide details on the timing and reason for any past trips to the USA?
What were the dates of your prior visit(s) to the United States, and what was its (their) purpose?

questions.question: Have you ever been denied a US visa?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{visa_refusal_history ? 'Yes, I was denied a US visa previously.' : 'No, I have never been denied a US visa.'}}
questions.guidance: Honesty is critical - this will be verified in your records.
questions.tooltip: Previous refusals are reviewed carefully.
variations: Has your application for a United States visa ever been refused?
Have you previously been informed that you were ineligible for a U.S. visa?
Was a United States visa application ever unsuccessful for you?
Have you ever received a denial for a visa to the United States?

questions.question: Explain the visa refusal and what has changed.
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{visa_refusal_explanation}} Since then, circumstances have significantly improved and I am now clearly eligible for F1 visa status.
questions.guidance: Honestly explain the reason and how circumstances have improved.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates addressing previous concerns.
variations: Could you please elaborate on the details of your previous visa denial and how your situation has since changed?
We would like to understand the circumstances surrounding your past visa refusal and what current developments address the concerns from that time.
Please provide an account of any prior visa rejections you may have experienced and explain what is different now.
Can you describe the reasons for your previous visa denial and outline what steps have been taken or what new information is relevant since then?

questions.question: Do you have any criminal convictions?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{criminal_record ? 'I need to disclose criminal history to USCIS.' : 'No, I do not have any criminal record.'}}
questions.guidance: Always answer truthfully - criminal history triggers security reviews.
questions.tooltip: Criminal history is grounds for visa denial.
variations: Have you ever been found guilty of a criminal offense?
Do you have a history of any criminal convictions or legal judgments against you?
Could you confirm if you have any arrests or convictions on your record?
Have you ever been subject to a criminal prosecution that resulted in a conviction?

questions.question: Do you have any immigration or legal issues in the USA?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{any_us_immigration_issues ? 'I have relevant issues to disclose.' : 'No, I have no immigration or legal issues in the USA.'}}
questions.guidance: Disclose any DUI, arrests, or overstays if applicable.
questions.tooltip: Security clearance and admissibility requirement.
variations: Have you encountered any immigration complications or legal challenges within the United States?
Do you have any outstanding legal matters or immigration violations in the USA?
Have you ever faced any adverse immigration decisions or legal entanglements in the United States?
Are there any past or current legal or immigration issues connected to your presence in the U.S.?

questions.question: Have you ever overstayed a visa in any country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{previous_overstay ? 'I have had a previous visa overstay that I need to disclose.' : 'No, I have never overstayed a visa.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer truthfully - overstays are serious immigration violations.
questions.tooltip: Overstays can result in visa denials and bans.
variations: Have you ever remained in any country beyond the authorized period of your visa?
Have you ever exceeded the permitted duration of stay granted by a visa in any nation?
Is there any instance where you have overstayed your welcome on a visa in a foreign country?
Have you ever violated the terms of a visa by remaining past its expiration date in another country?

questions.question: Have you been denied a visa to any other country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{other_countries_visa_refusal ? 'Yes, I have been denied visas to other countries.' : 'No, I have not been denied visas to other countries.'}}
questions.guidance: Multiple refusals indicate patterns affecting F1 eligibility.
questions.tooltip: Visa refusals to other countries may impact F1 approval.
variations: Has your application for a visa ever been refused by any other country?
Have you previously received a visa denial from a different nation?
Is there a record of you being denied a visa by any foreign government?
Have you ever faced a visa rejection from another country's consulate or embassy?
